text: A civil appeal is a legal process by which a party challenges a lower court's decision in a civil matter before a higher court.
Jurisdiction is the authority of a court to hear and decide a case, including territorial and subject-matter jurisdiction.
The party who files an appeal against a court decision.
The party against whom an appeal is filed.
A written complaint filed in a civil court stating the cause of action.
The defendant's formal reply to the plaint.
A reply filed by the plaintiff to the defendant's written statement.
A formal expression of a civil court's final decision determining rights of parties.
The reasoning provided by a judge explaining the decision.
Rejection of a case by a court without granting relief.
Legal reasons on which a judgment is challenged in a higher court.
Material presented in court to prove or disprove facts.
A formal communication informing a party of a legal claim or demand.
A civil suit filed to recover money owed.
Additional amount payable on a principal sum, often awarded by courts.
A document attached to a legal filing as supporting evidence.
A court order requiring a party to appear and respond to a case.
The legally prescribed time limit for filing a case or appeal.
Fees paid to the court for filing legal proceedings.
A declaration confirming that the contents of a legal document are true.
The set of facts that give rise to a legal claim.
The remedy sought by a party from the court.
The court of first instance where a case is initially heard.
A court that reviews decisions of lower courts.
The obligation to prove allegations in a case.
The level of certainty required to prove a case (preponderance in civil cases).
Questioning of a witness by the opposing party.
A written statement sworn under oath used as evidence.
The process of enforcing a court's decree.
A court order temporarily suspending enforcement of a judgment.
A court order directing a party to do or refrain from doing something.
Expenses awarded by the court to compensate a party for litigation.
Formal written statements of claims and defenses.
Initial testimony given by a witness.
Evidence in the form of documents.
Evidence given verbally by witnesses in court.
A statement acknowledging a fact as true.
A statement rejecting an allegation made by the opposing party.
A point of dispute between parties that the court must decide.
A decision by the court on a particular issue.
A legal principle established in earlier cases that courts follow.
The legal reasoning forming the basis of a judgment.
Remarks made by a judge that are not essential to the decision.
The means by which a court enforces a right or compensates a loss.
A legally binding agreement between parties.
Failure to perform obligations under a contract.
Monetary compensation awarded for loss or injury.
A court order requiring a party to fulfill contractual obligations.
A contractual obligation to compensate for loss.
A dispute resolution method where parties submit to a neutral arbitrator for a binding decision.
A legal contract by which a landlord grants a tenant the right to occupy property for a specified period in exchange for rent.
The landlord or owner who grants a lease to a tenant.
The tenant who receives the right to occupy property under a lease agreement.
Periodic payment made by a tenant to a landlord for the use of property.
An amount paid by a tenant to a landlord as security against damage or unpaid rent, refundable at the end of tenancy.
The legal process by which a landlord removes a tenant from the property.
A formal notice issued by a landlord requiring a tenant to vacate the premises.
The legal right to occupy property owned by another person, typically under a lease or rental agreement.
Unpaid rent that is overdue and owed by a tenant to a landlord.
When a tenant rents out all or part of the leased property to another person with or without landlord's permission.
An agreement under which a licensor grants a licensee permission to use premises for a fixed period without creating a tenancy.
A person who has been granted a licence to occupy or use premises.
The owner who grants a licence to another person to use their property.
Physical control or occupancy of a property.
Delivery of a property free from any occupants or encumbrances to the new owner or tenant.
Profits or compensation recoverable from a person who has wrongfully occupied property after their right to possession has ended.
A legal action to recover possession of land or property from an occupant.
Statutory regulation limiting the amount a landlord may charge for renting out property, governed by state Rent Control Acts in India.
The maximum rent fixed under rent control legislation that a landlord is permitted to charge.
A tenancy that may be terminated at any time by either the landlord or tenant without notice.
A legal document that transfers ownership of immovable property from seller to buyer upon receipt of full consideration.
The legal transfer of property title from one person to another.
A transfer of interest in property to a lender as security for repayment of a loan.
The borrower who pledges property as security for a loan.
The lender who holds the mortgage on the property as security.
A claim, lien, or liability attached to a property that may affect its transfer.
A legal document proving ownership of property.
The process of recording a legal document with the government authority to give it legal validity, mandatory for property transactions above a threshold value.
A tax levied by the state government on legal documents including property transactions, calculated as a percentage of property value.
A legal document authorizing one person to act on behalf of another in legal or financial matters.
The division of jointly owned property among co-owners, either by mutual agreement or court order.
A person who shares ownership of property with one or more other persons.
A form of co-ownership where all owners have equal rights and the right of survivorship.
A form of co-ownership where each owner holds a distinct share that can be transferred independently.
A legal document transferring ownership of property voluntarily without any consideration.
A legal declaration of a person's wishes regarding the distribution of their property after death.
The legal process of proving a will is valid and administering the estate of a deceased person.
The legal process by which a person's property passes to heirs after death.
Dying without a valid will, in which case property is distributed according to personal law.
The legal right to inherit property from a deceased person.
Financial support ordered by a court to be paid by one party to another, typically in matrimonial or family disputes.
Periodic financial support paid by one spouse to another after separation or divorce.
The legal dissolution of a marriage by a court.
Dissolution of marriage by agreement of both spouses under Section 13B of the Hindu Marriage Act or Section 28 of the Special Marriage Act.
The legal right and responsibility to care for and make decisions about a child.
A temporary court order made during the pendency of proceedings to maintain the status quo or provide urgent relief.
A court proceeding or order made in the absence of one party.
A formal notice filed in court requesting that no action be taken without informing the person who filed it.
Willful disobedience or disrespect of a court order or authority.
A court decree passed on the basis of a settlement reached between the parties during litigation.
A written contract between landlord and tenant specifying terms of rental including rent amount, duration, and obligations of both parties.
The owner of property who lets it out to a tenant in exchange for rent.
A person who occupies property owned by another under a rental or lease agreement.
The fixed amount payable by a tenant to the landlord every month for use of the premises.
An amount paid by the tenant at the start of tenancy, returnable upon vacating the premises subject to deductions for damages.
Periodic charges paid for upkeep and maintenance of common areas and building facilities, typically collected by the housing society.
A pre-agreed percentage by which monthly rent is escalated each year, typically 5-10% in Indian residential agreements.
A fine imposed on the tenant for failure to pay rent within the stipulated due date, usually charged per day of delay.
A clause restricting use of the leased premises exclusively for living and prohibiting any commercial activity.
Major repairs to the core structure of a building which are the responsibility of the landlord and not the tenant.
The tenant's right to use and enjoy the rented premises without interference or disturbance from the landlord.
The landlord's right to inspect the premises with prior notice, typically 24-48 hours in advance.
A written notice given by either party to end a rental agreement, typically 30 days in advance.
Payment of rent via electronic bank transfer as specified in the rental agreement.
The mechanism agreed upon to resolve conflicts arising from the agreement, including negotiation and recourse to competent court.
The law applicable to a contract, in this context the law of the State of Maharashtra.
A person who signs a legal document to attest that the parties signed it in their presence.
Bye-laws and regulations of a housing cooperative society that the tenant is obligated to follow.
The net usable floor area of an apartment excluding the area covered by walls, as measured in square feet or square meters.
The court having proper jurisdiction to adjudicate disputes arising from the agreement, typically the District Court of the relevant city.
In a lease or rent agreement, the lessor is the landlord (पट्टादाता) who grants the property on rent. Must NOT be translated as beneficiary or labharthi.
In a lease or rent agreement, the lessee is the tenant (पट्टेदार) who takes the property on rent.
Also called किरायानामा or भाड़ा पट्टा करार in Hindi. A contract between landlord and tenant for residential tenancy.
Called सुरक्षा जमा राशि in Hindi. Refundable amount paid by tenant to landlord at start of tenancy.
Called शांतिपूर्ण कब्जा in Hindi legal usage. The tenant's right to peaceful occupation without landlord interference.
